questions.question: Look at paragraph 18. What happens as a result of people not having a common language and not knowing the area?

responses.content:
cell phone comes out of a pocket. Within seconds, the rest of the world’s five billion phones follow. All of them—even those compatible with the region’s towers—are displaying some version of “NO SIGNAL”. The cell networks have all collapsed under the unprecedented load.

(14) The T. F. Green airport in Warwick, Rhode Island handles a few thousand passengers a day. Assuming they got things organized (including sending out scouting missions to retrieve fuel), they could run at 500% capacity for years without making a dent in the crowd.

(15) The addition of all the nearby airports doesn’t change the equation much. Nor does the region’s light rail system. Crowds climb on board container ships in the deepwater port of Providence, but stocking sufficient food and water for a long sea voyage proves a challenge.
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's highlight is much too long and includes multiple sentences from different paragraphs, rather than a single sentence or phrase that shows a cause-and-effect relationship. The student's response exceeds the character limit and does not meet the requirements of the question.

responses.content:
Rhode Island’s half-million cars are commandeered. Moments later, I-95, I-195, and I-295 become the sites of the largest traffic jam in the history of the planet. Most of the cars are engulfed by the crowds, but a lucky few get out and begin wandering the abandoned road network.

(17) Some make it past New York or Boston before running out of fuel. Since the electricity is probably not on at this point, rather than find a working gas pump, it’s easier to just abandon the car and steal the new one. Who can stop you? All the cops are in Rhode Island.

questions.question: What is the central idea of “Everybody Jump”?

responses.content: The edge of the crowd spread outward into the south Massachusetts and Connecticut. Any two people were unlikely to have a language in common, almost nobody knows the area  Violence is common. Everybody is hungry and thirsty. Grocery stores are empty. Fresh water is hard to come by, and there's no effective system to distribute. Within weeks, Rhode Island is a graveyard of billions.
responses.bucket_rationale: The response accurately identifies multiple consequences from paragraph 18, including violence, hunger, thirst, empty grocery stores, and lack of fresh water. It also mentions the spread of the crowd and the lack of a common language, which are key causes. The answer matches the meaning of the text and does not contradict any key scene elements. The language is appropriate and the response is text-specific.
